caption: The market's average annual return over the last 50 years is about 10%. You don't need to time it or beat it — you need to be in it, consistently, for a long time.
Old money isn't about the amount — it's about time in the market. A modest amount invested young and left alone for 30+ years usually beats a much larger amount invested late.

A 750 credit score at 19 isn't luck. It's one card, paid off in full, every single month, for two years straight, with utilization under 10%. Payment history alone is 35% of the score. Start now and the number takes care of itself.

An emergency fund isn't exciting, but 3 months of essential expenses sitting in a high-yield savings account is the difference between a flat tire being an inconvenience and it being a crisis. Start with $500. Build from there.
Every share of stock you own makes you a legal part-owner of that company. Buy one share of a company you actually believe in and you stop being just a customer.
Entry-level financial analyst roles start around $60-75K in most major markets. It's not the ceiling, it's the door in. Learn the terminal, learn the model, and the title stops being aspirational.

A single share of an S&P 500 index fund gives you partial ownership in Amazon, Apple, Microsoft, and 497 other companies at once. You don't need $10,000 to own a piece of corporate America. You need an account and $50.
based_on_format: real-fact flex line over cinematic footage (bigclaytz-style)

caption: Locked in isn't a mood, it's a decision. Same amount of hours in the day as everyone else — the difference is what you do with them.
No plan B means plan A has to work — so you build the habits now that make sure it does. Start before you feel ready.
Untouchable isn't luck. It's what it looks like when the work already got done before anyone was watching.
based_on_format: cinematic aesthetic edit, fast NYC finance-district cuts (riskanddreward/ole_pters-style)
